title: strychnos vanprukii var. acuminata
intro: Strychnos vanprukii var. acuminata, also known as the Thailand poison nut or Thai poison nut, is a plant species in the family Loganiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloid content, which includes strychnine and brucine. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential use as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds and leaves are often dried and powdered, then used to make teas or infusions, although its use requires caution and should be strictly controlled due to its toxic alkaloids.
